name: Strait of Hormuz
summary: Day 72 Sunday. **OPERATIONAL REGIME PERSISTING — Lloyd's List Hormuz transit ~6 vessels/day vs ~60 baseline (latest data point); ~1,500 commercial vessels remain anchored or holding in Persian Gulf; CENTCOM precision tally May 9: 58 vessels redirected + 4 Iranian-flagged tankers disabled since April 13 (CONFIRMED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing). The blockade is in its 27th day with rising operational tempo (4 disabled tankers in 4 days).** **B1 BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING — FIRST COMMERCIAL VESSEL ATTACK SINCE CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO (MAY 5) AND FIRST NEAR QATAR'S COAST.** Well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan; small fire extinguished, no casualties; UKMTO Warning notice posted; vessel flag/owner not publicly disclosed; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY (consistent with May 5 'claim-management discrimination pattern'). Operationally consistent with IRGC signal to Qatar that mediator role does not buy immunity for blockade-aligned shipping. **B2 KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK (NEW — first hard-data signal blockade biting at production-export interface):** Sentinel-1/2/3 imagery May 6-8 shows large slick west of Kharg (~45km surface extent); Iran (lawmaker via Iran International/Maariv) blames European tanker waste; Western analysts (Fox-quoted) suggest blockade pressure forcing storage-overflow dumping. CAUSE CONTESTED but the slick is operationally confirmed (CONFIRMED satellite imagery — Copernicus, Anadolu, JPost, NewsNation, France 24). **MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE UNCHANGED — no new statement; institutional intelligence durable.** This is the cleanest possible operational answer to yesterday's question of which track wins (operational reality vs MOU 'both sides lift blockades' rhetoric) — direction is overwhelmingly toward the PGSA regime, not the MOU regime (CONFIRMED — Lloyd's List Intelligence via CNN, Gulf News, HormuzStraitMonitor.com). The 1,500-vessel figure is the largest reported standstill of the cycle. **MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE INSTITUTIONALIZES SELECTIVE HORMUZ CONTROL AS PERMANENT STRATEGIC ASSET (NEW — MAJOR INSTITUTIONAL):** Mohammad Mokhber (adviser to supreme leader, described as Mojtaba's adviser): 'The Strait of Hormuz represents an opportunity as precious as an atomic bomb. Indeed, having in one's hands a position that allows you to influence the global economy with a single decision is a major opportunity.' Pledged Iran 'will change the [legal] regime of this strait — through international law if possible, and unilaterally if not' (CONFIRMED — Times of Israel, L'Orient Today, Middle East Eye, Deccan Herald). First time a leadership-adjacent figure has publicly endorsed unilateral codification of selective Hormuz control. Reads as institutional intelligence: even if MOU is signed, Iran intends to retain operational regime of Hormuz transit control as a permanent strategic asset — directly contradicts MOU's 'BOTH sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. **CENTCOM SECOND DAY OF TANKER STRIKES + BLOCKADE SCALE FORMALIZED:** US Navy F/A-18 from USS George H.W. Bush disabled M/T Sea Star III and M/T Sevda May 8 (precision-munition into smokestacks, same tactic as M/T Hasna May 7); CENTCOM disclosed 'more than 70 tankers' held under blockade, 20 warships, 200 aircraft, 15,000 troops enforcing — 'fully in effect' (CONFIRMED — UPI, Stars and Stripes, NPR, CBS, Fox News, TWZ). Iran claimed counter-seizure of unidentified tanker in Gulf of Oman. The trinary regime is the operating reality: US blockade fully in force, Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules at Hormuz, both sides engaging kinetically against each other's vessels, both sides claiming the ceasefire holds. Yesterday's 'love tap' framing did NOT survive a single 24-hour cycle. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day, 6-8x pre-war norm — insurers have NOT bought the May 5 pause as durable; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 3/10 — failing. Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden; UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED. Hormuz reopening trigger ('transit volume above 30/day for 3 consecutive days') failing harder — zero transits May 7.

active_triggers.condition: Hormuz reopens to all traffic (even partially)
US attempts naval escort or forced opening of Hormuz

War risk insurance for Gulf transits breaks below 0.5% hull value (normalization threshold) — REFRAMED 2026-05-06: 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% within 10 sessions' is now the diplomatic-track-durability test under the Trump pause framework
Iran formally reopens Hormuz to all traffic within 14-21 days of May 5 pause (by 2026-05-26) — would collapse tanker spot rates -15-25%
Second commercial vessel attack within 7 days inside Qatari EEZ following Sunday May 10 bulk carrier strike — confirms IRGC graduated-pressure-on-mediator-coastline pattern
Kharg Island slick re-appears on subsequent satellite passes (Copernicus Sentinel) within 14 days — confirms Iran storage-overflow / regime-dumping hypothesis vs European-tanker-waste counter-claim

Hormuz remains under Iranian selective control even within ceasefire framework — ACTIVE 2026-04-08: Iran's 'controlled passage via coordination with armed forces' framing in the ceasefire text formali
Physical-futures gap closes below $10/bbl (Dated Brent converges to Brent futures)

recent_signals.headline: BULK CARRIER STRUCK BY UNKNOWN PROJECTILE 23NM NE OF DOHA SUNDAY MORNING (NEW — FIRST COMMERCIAL VESSEL ATTACK SINCE CMA CGM SAN ANTONIO MAY 5; FIRST NEAR QATAR'S COAST; SMALL FIRE EXTINGUISHED, NO CASUALTIES; UKMTO WARNING NOTICE; NO CLAIM OF RESPONSIBILITY); KHARG ISLAND SATELLITE-CONFIRMED OIL SLICK MAY 6-8 (NEW — FIRST HARD-DATA SIGNAL BLOCKADE BITING AT PRODUCTION-EXPORT INTERFACE; CAUSE CONTESTED — Iran blames European tanker waste, Fox-quoted experts suggest storage-overflow dumping); CENTCOM PRECISION TALLY 58 VESSELS REDIRECTED + 4 IRANIAN-FLAGGED TANKERS DISABLED SINCE APRIL 13 (UPDATED — refines yesterday's >70-tankers-held framing; 4 disabled tankers: M/T Hasna May 7, M/T Sea Star III + M/T Sevda May 8, 4th identity not publicly disclosed); LLOYD'S LIST TRANSIT ~6 VESSELS/DAY VS ~60 BASELINE (latest data point); ~1,500 COMMERCIAL VESSELS ANCHORED. Bulk carrier strike geographic significance is acute: 23nm NE of Doha is well inside Qatar's EEZ, immediately offshore from Hamad Port and Ras Laffan, ordinarily considered the safest waters in the Persian Gulf. Three reading frames operationally consistent: (i) IRGC pressure-tactic on mediator coastline concurrent with al-Thani Miami meeting; (ii) Houthi-style proxy operation (LOW probability — wrong geometry); (iii) misfire/accidental engagement. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M / 7-day baseline (6-8x pre-war norm) — insurers have NOT bought peace story; trigger 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' Day 4 of 10 — failing harder. Brent close $101.29 May 8 (yfinance); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. Lloyd's reports ~10% of normal Hormuz throughput. UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED. The trinary regime is now stabilizing into sustainable operational pattern: US blockade fully in force, Iran selectively enforcing PGSA rules, both sides engaging kinetically against each other's vessels and now near mediator coastline.
sources: UKMTO Warning notice (bulk carrier near Doha)
Iran International (Iran lawmaker denial via Maariv)
recent_signals.verification: Bulk carrier strike near Doha = CONFIRMED ACTION (UKMTO primary, multi-source); perpetrator UNKNOWN (no party has claimed); Kharg satellite slick = CONFIRMED imagery (Copernicus + multiple Western corroborators); CAUSE CONTESTED (Iran lawmaker via Iran International/Maariv vs Fox News-quoted unnamed experts — neither side independently confirmed); CENTCOM 58/4 tally = CONFIRMED ACTION (CENTCOM primary, multi-source); Lloyd's List transit + 1,500 anchored = CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL FACT (Lloyd's + CNN); AWRP unchanged = CONFIRMED multi-source baseline

recent_signals.headline: ZERO HORMUZ TRANSITS MAY 7 + ~1,500 VESSELS ANCHORED IN PERSIAN GULF (largest standstill of cycle); CENTCOM DISCLOSED >70 TANKERS HELD UNDER BLOCKADE + 20 WARSHIPS, 200 AIRCRAFT, 15,000 TROOPS ENFORCING; SECOND DAY OF US TANKER STRIKES (M/T SEA STAR III + M/T SEVDA, MAY 8); MOKHBER 'ATOMIC-BOMB' DOCTRINE INSTITUTIONALIZES SELECTIVE HORMUZ CONTROL AS PERMANENT STRATEGIC ASSET. The 7→0 transit count is a meaningful operational deterioration. AWRP unchanged at ~1% H&M (6-8x pre-war), 'AWRP normalization toward 0.5% by May 16' trigger Day 3/10 — failing. Mokhber (described as Mojtaba's adviser): 'Strait of Hormuz represents an opportunity as precious as an atomic bomb'; pledged Iran 'will change the [legal] regime of this strait — through international law if possible, and unilaterally if not' — first leadership-adjacent voice publicly endorsing unilateral codification of selective Hormuz control as permanent strategic asset; substantively contradicts MOU's 'BOTH sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision. CENTCOM blockade scale formally disclosed for first time: '20 warships stocked with over 200 aircraft and 15,000 troops are carrying out the blockade, which remains fully in effect.' Brent close $101.73 May 8 (+1.66% 1D); Friday range $96.07 → $103.96 = trinary-regime signature oscillation. France Charles de Gaulle CSG remains pre-positioned in Red Sea / Gulf of Aden — UK-France conditional Hormuz mission framework UNCHANGED at 51-nation Paris summit (April 17) framework.
sources: CNN (citing Lloyd's List + anonymous shipping industry source on 1,500-vessel figure)
USNI / NBC / Defense News / Naval News (French CSG status)
recent_signals.verification: Hormuz transit data + 1,500 vessel anchored = CONFIRMED OPERATIONAL FACT; Mokhber 'atomic-bomb' speech = CONFIRMED RHETORIC/THREAT (institutional, multi-source primary); CENTCOM blockade scale + tanker disabling = CONFIRMED ACTION (CENTCOM primary, multi-source); AWRP unchanged = CONFIRMED multi-source baseline; French CSG = CONFIRMED PREPARATION POSTURE (no operational deployment to Hormuz)

recent_signals.headline: MOU FRAMEWORK INCLUDES EXPLICIT 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES'; IRAN SIGNALED FIRST UNCONDITIONAL REOPENING OFFER OF CYCLE — PBS/AP/AL JAZEERA cite Iranian officials offering to 'reopen Strait of Hormuz if US lifts blockade and war ends'; IRGC Navy added 'safe transit ensured with new procedures.' BUT operational reality lags: 5-6 vessels/day vs 138 norm; AWRP at 0.8-1% (still 6-8x baseline); CMA CGM San Antonio struck May 5 by cruise missile (CBS-attribution to Iran, IRGC has NOT claimed). France Charles de Gaulle CSG transits Suez to Red Sea/Gulf of Aden — first European naval shift of cycle; Macron called for Hormuz reopening AND spoke with Pezeshkian. Brent collapsed -7% May 6 to ~$103 on MOU scoop; Goldman Q2 cut to $90; World Bank $86 average for 2026. VLCC TD3C steepest 1-day drop in 5+ years on MOU news ('lead balloon' — Lloyd's List).
recent_signals.verification: MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision = REPORTED (Axios, multi-corroborated); Iran reopening offer = REPORTED→CONFIRMED (PBS/AP direct quotes); Operational data (transit, AWRP, VLCC) = CONFIRMED (Lloyd's List, Strauss Center, Hormuz tracker); CMA CGM strike = CONFIRMED action, CLAIMED Iran attribution; French CSG = CONFIRMED (French armed forces primary)

historical_summaries.summary: 12 signals. IRGC announced Hormuz closed to US, Israeli, and Western allied ships | Dallas Federal Reserve described closure as 'largest disruption to energy supply since the 1970s energy crisis' and 'largest in the history | Mojtaba Khamenei's first statement confirmed Hormuz would remain closed to pressure Iran's enemies

64 signals. WSJ paradigm shift CORROBORATED by CNN: 'Trump administration believes it will have to end Iran war with oil chokepoint still closed | Al-Salmi VLCC attacked at Dubai port anchorage — 31 nautical miles NW of Dubai, OUTSIDE Hormuz proper | CONFIRMED: Hormuz toll law passed FULL Majlis (not just committee)
